pages.name: How to Level Up (60 Seconds at the Bonfire)

text.content:
Leveling happens only at the Firelink bonfire, where the Fire Keeper sits. It is designed to take about a minute. Here is the whole procedure:

The four steps

  1. Pay souls. Spend the banked-souls cost for your next level from the shared pool (the DM runs the Level Up macro, or just subtract it on the Party sheet).
  2. Bump your class level by 1. On your character sheet, raise your class to the new level. The 5e engine auto-applies the auto-backbone: more HP, proficiency-bonus scaling, and Extra Attack at level 5 for martials. You never build this by hand.
  3. Make your Ashen pick. Open the matching card below for this level and choose: drag an Attribute card (from the Levels compendium) onto your sheet, or drag a spell (from the Spells compendium). That's your level-up.
  4. Skill levels (6 & 9 only): also pick one Weapon Art from the Levels compendium. See the last page.

The one decision that matters

Every level you choose either +1 attribute OR a spell (some levels grant two picks). This is the heart of Ashen's leveling:

  • Fighters spend on attributes — HP, damage, poise. They get tanky and reliable, but never gain magic.
  • Casters spend on spells — powerful, scene-defining magic. But every spell is an attribute point they didn't take, so they stay fragile. Glass cannons by choice.

Anyone can cast if they meet the stat gate and carry the catalyst. The tradeoff is the build.

Soul cost curve (banked souls)

LevelCostRunning total
3 → 4500500
4 → 58001,300
5 → 61,2002,500
6 → 71,8004,300
7 → 82,6006,900
8 → 93,60010,500
9 → 105,00015,400

Boss kills cover roughly half of this; farming, the dragon, and hollows cover the rest.

Auto-backbone (free, every level)

  • Raise your class to level 4: +1 Hit Die of HP, sheet recalculates.

Your pick — choose ONE

  • +1 Attribute — drag any one Attribute card (Vigor, Strength, Dexterity, Endurance, Intelligence, Faith, Attunement).
  • OR a Tier-1 spell — drag any T1 sorcery, miracle, or pyromancy you qualify for (need 1 point in the gating stat + the catalyst in hand).

Tier available: Tier 1 only.

Note: standard 5e ability-score increases are OFF in Ashen. All growth comes from Attribute cards and spells.

Auto-backbone

  • Raise your class to level 5: +1 Hit Die of HP, proficiency bonus rises to +3.
  • Martials gain Extra Attack automatically — you now Strike twice with the Attack action. (If your sheet didn't add it, drop on the Extra Attack reminder card.)

Your pick — choose ONE

  • +1 Attribute, OR
  • a Tier-1 spell.

Tier available: Tier 1. (Tier 2 unlocks next level.)

pages.name: Level 5 → 6  (cost: 1,200 souls)  — TIER 2 UNLOCKS + Skill pick

text.content:
Auto-backbone

  • Raise your class to level 6: +1 Hit Die of HP.

Tier 2 is now available

If you have 3 points invested in Intelligence (sorceries) or Faith (miracles), or an upgraded Pyromancy Flame, you may now learn Tier-2 spells.

Your pick — this is a DOUBLE level

  • Take TWO picks: any combination of +1 attribute and/or spells from Tier 1–2. (e.g. two attributes, or one attribute + one spell, or two spells.)

+ Skill pick (Weapon Art)

Everyone also chooses one Weapon Art from the Levels compendium (see last page). Martial or caster — your choice.

Auto-backbone

  • Raise your class to level 7: +1 Hit Die of HP.

Your pick — choose ONE

  • +1 Attribute, OR
  • a spell from Tier 1–2.

Tier available: Tier 1–2.

Auto-backbone

  • Raise your class to level 8: +1 Hit Die of HP.

Your pick — choose ONE

  • +1 Attribute, OR
  • a spell from Tier 1–2.

Tier available: Tier 1–2. (Tier 3 unlocks next level.)

pages.name: Level 8 → 9  (cost: 3,600 souls)  — TIER 3 UNLOCKS + Skill pick

text.content:
Auto-backbone

  • Raise your class to level 9: +1 Hit Die of HP, proficiency bonus rises to +4.

Tier 3 is now available

With 5 points in Intelligence or Faith (or a fully upgraded Pyromancy Flame), you may learn Tier-3 spells — the scene-enders.

Your pick — choose ONE

  • +1 Attribute, OR
  • a spell from Tier 1–3.

+ Skill pick (Weapon Art)

Everyone chooses a second Weapon Art (see last page).

Auto-backbone

  • Raise your class to level 10: +1 Hit Die of HP.

Your pick — this is a DOUBLE level

  • Take TWO picks: any combination of +1 attribute and/or spells from Tier 1–3.

This is the build you take into Vordt. Casters: a fragile apex with two scene-enders. Fighters: a wall of HP, poise, and damage. Both are correct.

At levels 6 and 9 everyone also takes a Weapon Art — a juicy active trick. Drag the card from the Levels compendium. Martial and caster arts are both open to anyone.

Martial

  • Stomp — bonus-action shove + advantage on your next hit.
  • Perseverance — reaction: temp HP + stagger-immunity for a round.
  • Spin Slash — hit everyone adjacent at once.
  • Charge — rush in; prone on hit.
  • Quickstep — bonus-action 15-ft blink, no opportunity attacks.
  • Leo Riposte — your parry-ripostes deal +2 weapon dice.

Caster

  • Steady Chant — next spell uninterruptible, +2 DC.
  • Crystallize — 1/bonfire, a wholly-missed spell refunds its charges.
  • Pyromancer's Fervor — fire spells crit on 19–20.
  • Sage's Focus — 1/bonfire, cast a Tier-1 spell as a bonus action.

Universal

  • Estus Mastery — +1 Estus charge and quaff as a bonus action.
